title: 10 best herbal decoctions for eye swelling
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for eye swelling are a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to reduce puffiness and discomfort in the eyes. These decoctions, made by steeping herbs in hot water, help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing inflammation and swelling. For example, chamomile and calendula decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that can ease redness and itching, while peppermint and cucumber decoctions cool and constrict blood vessels to reduce puffiness. By using these herbal decoctions, individuals can enjoy improved eye health and reduced discomfort, allowing them to feel more refreshed and confident throughout the day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel decoctions helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in witch hazel have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease puffiness, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing irritated eyes. By applying a cooled compress of the decoction to the affected area, the warmth from the herb helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, providing rapid relief from eye strain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce puffiness and redness in the eyes by reducing swelling and inflammation caused by allergic reactions or irritation.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in the decoction neutralize free radicals, which can damage eye tissues and contribute to swelling and discoloration, helping to soothe and protect the delicate eye area.

Astringent: The astringent properties of the decoction help constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation and swelling around the eyes, and also provide a protective barrier against irritants and allergens.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: This tannin is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce swelling and soothe eye irritation.
Hamamelose: This sugar-like compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with eye swelling.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the eyes, thereby alleviating eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Buds: The buds are used due to their high concentration of hamamelitannin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Barks: The barks are used for their rich content of tannins and hamamelitannin, which help to reduce swelling and soothe the eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high tannin content and ability to constrict blood vessels, which helps to reduce eye swelling and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hamamelis virginiana bark and roots and store them in a cool dry place to preserve their potency.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried hamamelis virginiana bark and 1 teaspoon of dried roots for a standard decoction.
Combine the measured herbs with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 10 to 15 minutes to release the medicinal properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The decoction's antioxidants also help to soothe and calm the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, pot marigold contains flavonoids, which have been shown to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation, further helping to reduce swelling and dark circles under the eyes. Overall, pot marigold decoctions provide a natural and effective solution for alleviating eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold decoctions contain flavonoids and carotenoids that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, making them an effective natural remedy for conjunctivitis and other eye irritations.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold decoctions, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling and irritation.

Soothing and cooling properties: Pot marigold decoctions have a calming effect on the eyes, reducing redness, itchiness, and discomfort associated with eye swelling, making them a popular natural treatment for conjunctivitis and other eye irritations.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Flavonoids: Specifically, flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol present in Calendula have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to the reduction of eye swelling.
Carotenoids: Carotenoids like lutein and zeaxanthin in Calendula have antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, subsequently reducing eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are utilized for their astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which aid in reducing eye swelling and promoting healing.
Stems: The stems of Calendula officinalis are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate eye swelling and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon dried calendula officinalis flowers in a clean glass container for storage.
Combine 1 tablespoon dried calendula officinalis flowers with 1 cup boiling water in a heat-resistant glass cup.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 2 days or freeze for later use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Echinacea contains a variety of anti-inflammatory compounds, including alkamides and caffeic acid derivatives, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Echinacea's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling.
Immune-modulating effects: Echinacea has been shown to have immune-modulating effects, which may help reduce the severity of eye allergies and other conditions that cause swelling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with eye swelling because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory compounds, such as echinaceol and polyphenols. These natural agents work together to reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes. The decoction's antioxidant properties also help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthy circulation and reducing inflammation. As a result, herbal Kansas coneflower decoctions provide effective relief for eye swelling, making it a popular natural remedy for sinus pressure and allergies.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: Icariin, a flavonoid glycoside, helps reduce eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators.

Alkylamides (Cichoric acid and Alkylamide C): These phenolic compounds, particularly cichoric acid, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate eye swelling by scavenging free radicals and reducing inflammation.

Cichorin: Cichorin, a sesquiterpene lactone, exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that contribute to the reduction of eye swelling by regulating the immune response and preventing the release of pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain high concentrations of compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing eye swelling.
Leaves: The leaves have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, to alleviate eye swelling.
Barks: The barks of Echinacea angustifolia contain compounds that have been used to reduce inflammation and swelling, including in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots and rhizomes in the fall after the first frost to ensure potency.
Clean and dry the roots and rhizomes thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Chop the dried roots and rhizomes into small pieces to increase surface area and promote extraction.
Steep one to two teaspoons of chopped root and rhizome mixture in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove solids and obtain the liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with eye swelling because it contains a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and essential oils that work together to reduce inflammation and improve circulation in the delicate tissues around the eyes. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing puffiness and swelling, while its antioxidant effects protect the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals. This natural remedy is a safe and effective way to soothe and calm irritated or swollen eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant-rich properties of Ginkgo biloba decoctions scavenge free radicals that can contribute to eye swelling and oxidative stress, thereby protecting the delicate tissues around the eyes.
Vasodilatory property: The decoction's ability to dilate blood vessels may help improve circulation and reduce swelling in the eyes by reducing fluid buildup and promoting healthy drainage.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce eye swelling by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, thereby decreasing the severity of edema and promoting tissue repair.

Bilobalide: This triterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting vasodilation, which helps reduce fluid accumulation in the tissues.

Quercetin: As a potent flavonoid, quercetin exhibits strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that contribute to the reduction of eye swelling by scavenging free radicals, inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, and promoting vascular health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Seeds are used in decoctions due to their high content of bilobalide, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Barks: Barks are used in decoctions for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 to 100 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source in early fall.
Rinse the ginkgo biloba leaves in cold water to remove any impurities or debris.
Combine the ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 liter of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 30 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort decoctions helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbs contain flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the body. When used as an eye wash or applied topically to the affected area, St John's wort can help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from eye strain and fatigue, making it a popular choice for promoting healthy and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in St John's Wort decoctions have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Vasoactive properties: St John's Wort decoctions contain compounds that can help regulate blood flow to the eyes, reducing swelling by dilating or constricting blood vessels as needed.
remedy_constituents: Harpagide: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce swelling and soothe eye irritation.
Flowers: Contain bioflavonoids and anthocyanins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate eye swelling and promote healing.
Stems: May be used in decoctions for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce eye swelling and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a reputable source.
Boil 250 milliliters of water in a saucepan over high heat for 5 minutes.
Add the dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The soothing gel extracted from aloe vera contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that calm irritated tissues and constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid accumulation around the eyes. As a result, herbal aloe decoctions can provide quick relief from eye swelling, making them a popular remedy for reducing puffiness and promoting healthy, refreshed-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes by decreasing inflammation and soothing irritated tissues.
Antioxidant: Aloe vera's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the delicate eye tissues, reducing the severity of puffiness and swelling.
Vasodilatory: Aloe vera's vasodilatory properties help widen blood vessels in the eyes, allowing for improved circulation and reduced fluid retention, which contributes to a decrease in eye swelling.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds, particularly aloe-emodin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin in Aloe vera have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate eye swelling and promote healing.
Glycoproteins: The glycoproteins present in Aloe vera, such as aloin, have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm and reduce eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Most used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds such as aloe-emodin and aloin.

Barks: Utilized for their aloe-emodin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera leaves for decoction by selecting fresh green leaves from mature plants.
Cut the leaves into small pieces weighing about 2 ounces for every quart of water.
Combine the aloe vera pieces with 4 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to boil.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock decoctions helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The saponins present in burdock root have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, thereby reducing the swelling and discomfort associated with eye problems such as conjunctivitis or sinus pressure. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock root contains various polyphenolic compounds, such as caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the eyes, thereby alleviating puffiness and swelling.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in burdock decoctions neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to eye tissues, ultimately reducing swelling and promoting healthy vision.

remedy_constituents: Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin is a polysaccharide that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with eye conditions.
Arctiin: A phenolic compound, arctiin has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate eye swelling and promote overall eye health.

remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the eyes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of arctium lappa roots from a clean and dry location.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and mix with 500 milliliters of cold water.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh equisetum arvense roots or 2 tablespoons of dried roots in the spring or fall.
Wash the harvested roots thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned equisetum arvense roots into small pieces and add them to a pot.
Boil 2 cups of water and add the chopped roots to the pot for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids after the boiling process is complete.

remedy_recipe: Gather 30-60 grams of galactites tomentosa roots and rinse them with cold water to remove any impurities.
Chop the galactites tomentosa roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped galactites tomentosa roots with 1 liter of boiling water in a saucepan to create a decoction.
Simmer the decoction for 10-15 minutes to extract the active compounds from the galactites tomentosa roots.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids and discard the galactites tomentosa roots.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of gentiana lutea in late summer or early fall season.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces using a sharp knife for easier boiling.
Combine 4-8 grams of chopped roots with 1 liter of boiling water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 5-10 minutes then reduce heat to simmer for 15-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lonicera japonica flowers and add it to a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Heat 250 milliliters of water in a saucepan over medium flame for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for 20-25 minutes allowing the herbs to infuse fully.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup discarding the solids.
Let the decoction cool down to room temperature before refrigerating or using as needed.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in an amount of one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Measure and combine two cups of filtered water with the dried flower mixture in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture by placing it on a stovetop or in a microwave oven at high power for five to seven minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Allow the liquid to cool and use immediately or store in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves, weighing approximately 30-40 grams, for optimal potency and freshness.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove any impurities or debris.
Combine the rinsed leaves with one liter of boiling distilled water in a clean glass container, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a sterile glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid for later use.
Allow the prepared decoction to cool slightly before refrigerating or freezing for up to 3 days, with each dose consisting of 1-2 teaspoons taken as needed to alleviate eye swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Myrtle decoctions helps with eye swelling because its potent anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eyes. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh myrtus communis leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the harvested leaves with 200 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Bring the mixture to a boil using medium-high heat then reduce the temperature to low simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate clean glass container discarding the solids immediately.
Allow the cooled decoction to steep in the refrigerator at 4 degrees celsius for 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain decoctions helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and redness. Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain tannins, which help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of plantago major and measure out one tablespoon for every eight ounces.
Combine the measured plantago major leaves with four cups of water in a medium saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce to a simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh straining cloth into a clean glass container.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator and use within 24 hours.
